notes: Baji Rao II. The Treaty of Bassein (1802): It was signed between Lord Wellesley and Baji Rao II. Other Treaty: Treaty of Surat (1775) was signed between Raghunath Rao and the British. The Treaty of Salbai (1782) was signed between the Maratha Empire and the British East India Company.
Ranjit Singh. The Tripartite Treaty was signed by Shah Shujah, Ranjit Singh and Lord Auckland (Governor -General of India) in 1838.

Treaty of Amritsar - It was executed by the British East India Company and Raja Gulab Singh of Jammu after the First Anglo-Sikh War. Treaty of Allahabad : Signed in - 1765 between the Mughal emperor Shah Alam II and Lord Clive of the British East India Company. Treaty of Sugauli - signed between Nepal and the East India History Company in 1815 and ratified by 4 March 1816. Treaty of Purandar – Signed between Jai Singh I and Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj in 1665.
Paris. The Battle of Wandiwash was fought in Vandavasi, Tamil Nadu between the French and the British in 1760. This battle was part of the Third Carnatic War. The British defeated the French.

France. The Seven Years’ War, which took place between 1756-1763, was a global conflict that spanned five continents, though it was known in America as the ‘French and Indian War’. It solidified Britain’s stance as the most dominant European country in the world. Reason behind the Seven Years War - Both England and France wanted territories in North America.

Nadir Shah. Battle of Karnal (1739) : Fought between - Nadir Shah and Muhammad Shah. Victory - Nadir Shah easily outnumbered the Mughal forces and the Mughal emperor surrendered. Nadir Shah took the famous Kohinoor diamond from India and Shah Jahan's jewel studded peacock from the throne.

Battle of Plassey (1757). This battle was fought between the British East India Company led by Robert Clive and the Nawab of Bengal Siraj-ud-daula and his French allies. The Battle of Sindhuli was part of the unification of Nepal led by Prithvi Narayan Shah, King of Gorkha (later King of Nepal). Battle of Tiruvannamalai (1767) - Fought between the forces of the East India Company and the Kingdom of Mysore, led by Hyder Ali. Q.451. Which of the following Anglo-Maratha wars resulted in merging the Peshwas' territories with the Bombay Presidency? SSC CHSL 02/08/2023 (1st shift) (a) Fourth (b) Third (c) Second (d) First Sol.451.(b) Third (1817-1819). Anglo -Maratha war (The Great Maratha War) - The War was fought over territorial disputes, political dominance, and control over resources. First (1775-1782) - The Marathas won, Second (1803-1805) - The British won.

Battle of Buxar (1764) : It was fought between the forces of the British East India Company (led by Hector Munro) and the combined army of an alliance of Shuja-ud-Daulah, Mughal Emperor Shah Alam II and Mir Qasim. The Treaty of Allahabad (1765) was signed after this Battle.

The Treaty of Mangalore (1784). The second Anglo-Mysore War (1780 - 84) was fought by Hyder Ali, the Nizam and the Marathas against the British. Anglo-Mysore Wars : First (1767 - 69) - Between Hyder Ali and the British, concluded with the Treaty of Madras.Third (1790 - 92) - The British, the Nizam and the Marathas fought together against Tipu Sultan, concluded with the Treaty of Seringapatam. Fourth (1798 - 99) - Between Arthur Wellesley and Tipu Sultan (Died).

Nadir Shah. The Battle of Karnal - Fought between Nadir Shah (Persian Empire of the Afsharid dynasty) and Muhammad Shah (Mughal Emperor) in 1739. Nadir Shah emerged victorious in the battle. He took the famous Koh - i - Noor diamond and Shah Jahan's jewel-studded Peacock Throne. He was the founder of the Afsharid dynasty of Iran.
Shahalam II (Ali Gauhar) - Son of Alamgir II. Third Battle of Panipat (14 January, 1761): Between Maratha Empires and Ahmad Shah Abdali. (Victory - Ahmad Shah Abdali).
Ibrahim Lodhi. The Lodi dynasty (1451 - 1526, an Afghan dynasty was the fifth and final dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.
Battle of Aliwal (1846) - British (led by Sir Harry Smith) defeated Sikh forces (led by Ranjodh Singh Majithia).

Both I and II. Battle of Plassey (23 June 1757) - Bengal (led by Siraj ud-Daulah - last independent Nawab of Bengal) annexed by the British East India Company (led by Robert Clive - First British Governor of the Bengal Presidency).

1799. He is known as Tiger of Mysore. Tipu Sultan was inspired by the Ideals of the French Revolution. Daria Daulat Bagh is known as Tipu Sultan’s Summer Palace. He was finally defeated at the hands of Lord Wellesley.

British. The Siege of Arcot was the battle between British and combined forces of French and Nawab of Arcot. Robert Clive captured the Arcot after the battle. The war ended with the Treaty of Pondicherry in 1754 which made Mohammad Ali Khan Walajah as Nawab of Arcot.
1919. The defeated Germany after the First World War signed the Treaty of Versailles on 28 June 1919.

Assam. Treaty of Yandabo was the peace treaty that resulted at the end of the First Anglo Burmese War. It was signed between the British and Burmese. Q.463. In 1818, the Holkar Dynasty signed the Treaty of Mandsaur with the ______. SSC MTS 27/10/2021 (Evening) (a) Danish (b) Dutch (c) British (d) French Sol.463.(c) British. After Malhar Rao II was defeated in the battle of Mahidpur (1817) the treaty of Mandsaur was signed.

Battle of Chinhat. In the Battle of Chinhat, Barkat Ahmad was declared Chief Army Officer of the rebels. The Battle of Chinhat was fought on the morning of June 1857, between British forces and Indian rebels.

Portuguese. The battle took place on 29–30 November 1612 off the coast of Suvali, a village near Surat city. This relatively small naval battle is historically important as it marked the beginning of the end of Portugal’s commercial monopoly over India and the beginning of the ascent of the English East India Company’s presence in India.
25 November 1759, during the Seven Years' War between a force of British troops mainly of the British East India Company and a force of the Dutch East India Company.
1849. This war resulted in the fall of the Sikh Empire and The East India Company annexed Punjab and it became part of North West Frontier Province.

Siraj-ud-Daula . The Treaty of Alinagar was signed on 9 February 1757 between Robert Clive of the British East India Company and the Nawab of Bengal, Mirza Muhammad Siraj Ud Daula. Based on the terms of the accord, the Nawab would recognize all the provisions of Mughal Emperor Farrukhsiyar farman of 1717.
